claimText: Tropical cyclones require a sea surface temperature greater than 26°C to form.
evidenceExcerpt: Always form over oceans where sea surface temperature is greater than 26°C.
examUse: Statement-based question on conditions for cyclone formation.

claimText: Tropical cyclones develop at latitudes usually greater than 5° from the equator due to the necessity of the Coriolis force.
evidenceExcerpt: Develop at latitudes usually greater than 5° from equator in the presence of Coriolis force.
examUse: Statement-based question on the role of Coriolis force and equatorial absence of cyclones.

claimText: Tropical cyclones are known by different regional names, such as Hurricanes in the North Atlantic, Typhoons in the Western North Pacific, and Willy-willies in the Eastern part of the Southern Indian Ocean.
evidenceExcerpt: Terms for Tropical Cyclones: Hurricane – North Atlantic, Eastern North Pacific and South Pacific Ocean. Cyclone – Indian Ocean. Typhoon – Western North Pacific Ocean. Willy-willy: Eastern part of the Southern Indian Ocean.
examUse: Correct-pair matching question (Cyclone name vs. Region).
